chunk_summary: Guidelines for safe home workflows for fortifying human milk for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants, focusing on hygiene, preparation timing, and feeding strategies to prevent errors and ensure optimal nutrition post-discharge.
learning_objectives: Understand the criteria for using multicomponent fortification in preterm or VLBW infants post-discharge.
Learn safe workflows for preparing and administering fortified human milk at home.
Identify common errors in home fortification and strategies to mitigate them.
Recognize the importance of hygiene and storage practices in reducing infection risks.
key_facts: Multicomponent fortification is selectively used for very preterm (<32 weeks) or VLBW infants when growth and phenotype justify it.
Fortified expressed milk should ideally be prepared immediately before feeding to avoid increased osmolality.
Model A: Pre-breastfeed supplement dose involves mixing 1 g fortifier into 5–10 mL freshly expressed milk and administering before breastfeeding.
Model B: Batch fortification requires refrigeration and use within 24 hours; avoid in high infection-risk homes or unreliable refrigeration.
Hygiene standards include handwashing, measured containers, labeling preparation times, and avoiding repeated rewarming of milk.
reference_quote: Several neonatal network protocols warn that preparing fortified expressed milk in advance and rewarming can increase osmolality, so families should prepare supplements immediately before feeding whenever possible.
document_key_topics: Post-discharge feeding strategies for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants
Home workflows for human milk fortification
Hygiene and safety in milk preparation and storage
